pr.title: allow for specific ifdef preservation in ctx
pr.author: thefoxcam

summary: Reworked tools/m2ctx/m2ctx.py so the pcpp path uses a custom Preprocessor subclass instead of invoking pcpp with a static command-line flag list. The custom preprocessor preserves selected conditional blocks keyed on M2C while still defining M2CTX for context generation, carrying over the original include paths, defines, undefines, compression/comment behavior, and MWERKS compatibility assumptions. Header guards and inline/type helper conditionals across effect, fighter, ground, item, menu, and toy headers were switched from #ifdef M2CTX to #ifdef M2C so those decomp.me/m2c-specific alternatives can survive into ctx output rather than being eagerly resolved during context preprocessing.
what_changed: Replaced the static PCPP_FLAGS list and subprocess-based pcpp invocation with an in-process pcpp Preprocessor subclass named CmdPreprocessor.
The custom pcpp path preserves conditionals for a passthrough keyword list containing M2C, while still defining M2CTX and __MWERKS__ and undefining FIGHTERVARS_SIZE.

Moved the pcpp behavior previously represented by flags such as passthru comments, line directive suppression, compression, include paths, defines, and undefines into Python code on the Preprocessor instance.
Added custom unknown-macro hooks for defined expressions, normal expressions, and macro-function expressions so passthru keywords can return None and be passed through as expanded as possible.
Added directive handling to avoid executing side-effecting #define/#undef directives while inside passthrough #if expressions, except for likely include-guard handling.
Changed several header conditionals from M2CTX to M2C for m2c/decomp.me-specific GObj wrapper typedefs and GET_* inline casts.
Affected header helpers include GET_EFFECT, GET_FIGHTER, GET_TOY, Fighter_GObj, Ground_GObj, Item_GObj, MenuInfo_GObj, and Menu_GObj conditionals.

The PR body states that ctx output was diffed against the old method and the only non-M2CTX_DUAL preservation difference observed was NOT_IMPLEMENTED from src/placeholder.h changing from an empty define to asm { nop }, claimed safe because it is unused.
smart_moves: Used a narrow passthrough macro strategy rather than preserving every unknown conditional, limiting context churn to intended m2c-facing branches.
Kept the existing context-generation environment explicit in the tool: same include directories, __MWERKS__, M2CTX, and FIGHTERVARS_SIZE undef behavior are represented in code.
Separated the macro that means 'building the ctx' from the macro that means 'preserve this branch for m2c/decomp.me consumers' by renaming affected guards from M2CTX to M2C.
Added custom pcpp hooks instead of relying only on command-line passthrough flags, allowing finer control over unknown macro expressions and side-effecting directives.
Validated the generated ctx against the previous method and documented the observed output delta in the PR body.
decomp_lessons: Do not overload M2CTX for both context-generation-time decisions and branches that need to remain visible to m2c/decomp.me; use a dedicated passthrough macro such as M2C for the latter.

When ctx generation needs to preserve specific #if/#ifdef blocks, in-process pcpp customization can be safer than broad passthru flags because hooks can distinguish selected macros, undefines, and directive side effects.
Context-generation tooling changes should be regression-checked by diffing old and new ctx output; small unrelated preprocessor differences should be called out explicitly.
Header conditionals that introduce decompiler-only wrapper structs or casts can be intentionally preserved into ctx output when external tools need to preprocess or choose those branches later.

If preserving conditional blocks, be careful not to execute #define or #undef directives inside unknown/passthrough conditions, since that can leak branch-local state into the rest of the generated context.
naming_conventions: M2CTX remains the macro for m2ctx context generation.
M2C is used as the preserved macro for m2c/decomp.me-facing conditional code in headers.
CmdPreprocessor is the local custom pcpp Preprocessor subclass used by tools/m2ctx/m2ctx.py.
Passthrough macro names are collected in self.passthru_keywords; in this PR the evidence shows M2C as the only entry.
Existing GET_* helper macro naming was retained: GET_EFFECT, GET_FIGHTER, and GET_TOY.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Preserving selected M2C conditionals in ctx lets downstream tools such as m2c or decomp.me apply their own preprocessing API instead of consuming a fully flattened ctx.
The changed headers keep alternate GObj typedefs and HSD_GObjGetUserData casts available behind M2C, which can help decompiler-context consumers see the forms intended for matching work.
The PR body notes a NOT_IMPLEMENTED define output difference, from empty to asm { nop }, but says this should not affect matching because it is unused.
review_feedback: No review comments were present in the provided excerpt.
The available human text is the PR body, which explains the intent, preservation target, carried-over flags, and ctx diff validation.
searchable_terms: allow for specific ifdef preservation in ctx
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that add more entries to m2ctx.py passthru_keywords beyond M2C.
Search for remaining #ifdef M2CTX in headers to check whether any are still overloaded for m2c/decomp.me behavior.
Look for later ctx-generation regressions involving pcpp custom Preprocessor hooks or preserved conditionals.
Search PRs mentioning M2CTX_DUAL to understand how it relates to the M2C passthrough introduced here.
Compare generated ctx output around src/placeholder.h NOT_IMPLEMENTED after this PR if matching or preprocessing issues appear.
